title: Write decimals and fractions shown on grids
description: Practice these problems to see how decimals and fractions can represent the same number.
topic_path: Write decimals and fractions shown on grids

assessment_items.question_content:
The square below represents one whole.

**Express the shaded area as both a fraction and a decimal.**



[[☃ image 1]]

**Fraction:**     [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

**Decimal:** [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
The square is split into $10$ equal pieces. 

$\blue{3}$ of the $10$ pieces are shaded.

We can write the fraction as $\blue{\dfrac{3}{10}}$ .
$\blue{\dfrac{3}{10}}$ can also be expressed as $\blue{3 \text{ tenths}}$.


Let's use a place value chart to write $\blue{3 \text{ tenths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens$~~~~~$|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-:
|$0$|$.$  | $\blue{3}$ |

We can express the shaded area in the following ways:

* Fraction:  $\dfrac{3}{10}$

* Decimal: $0.3$

options.alt: A square divided into ten equal-sized columns. The left three columns are shaded blue.

assessment_items.question_content:
The big square below represents one whole.

**Express the shaded area as both a fraction and a decimal.**



[[☃ image 1]]

**Fraction:**     [[☃ numeric-input 1]]

**Decimal:** [[☃ numeric-input 2]]
hints:
The big square is split into $100$ equal pieces. 


Let's count how many pieces are shaded.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\overbrace{\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}}^{\blueD{50}}+\blueD{6}=\blueD{56}$

$\blueD{56}$ of the $100$ pieces are shaded. 
We can write the fraction as $\blueD{\dfrac{56}{100}}$ .
$\blueD{\dfrac{56}{100}}$ can also be expressed as $\blueD{56 \text{ hundredths}}$.


Let's use a place value chart to write $\blueD{56 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$0$|$.$  | $\blueD{5}$ |$\blueD{6}$

We can express the shaded area in the following ways:

* Fraction:  $\dfrac{56}{100}$

* Decimal: $0.56$

options.alt: A square divided into 100 equal sized squares that are set in 10 rows of 10 squares each. The bottom 5 rows are shaded blue. In the sixth row, the left 6 squares are shaded.

hints:
The big square is split into $100$ equal pieces. 


Let's count how many pieces are shaded.



[[☃ image 1]]

$\overbrace{\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}+\blueD{10}}^{\blueD{30}}+\blueD{9}=\blueD{39}$

$\blueD{39}$ of the $100$ pieces are shaded. 
We can write the fraction as $\blueD{\dfrac{39}{100}}$ .
$\blueD{\dfrac{39}{100}}$ can also be expressed as $\blueD{39 \text{ hundredths}}$.


Let's use a place value chart to write $\blueD{39 \text{ hundredths}}$ as a decimal.

Tens|Ones | $.$ | Tenths | Hundredths
:-:|:-: | :-: | :-: | :-:
|$0$|$.$  | $\blueD{3}$ |$\blueD{9}$

We can express the shaded area in the following ways:

* Fraction:  $\dfrac{39}{100}$

* Decimal: $0.39$
